trade.summary: Substantial bilateral trade within the EU single market. Francia exports pharmaceuticals, aerospace products, and automobiles to Svezia. Svezia exports machinery, vehicles (Volvo, Scania), and paper products to Francia. Both countries have strong industrial bases with complementary strengths.

diplomatic.status: close EU allies and NATO partners
diplomatic.summary: Francia and Svezia are EU members and NATO allies (Svezia joined in 2024). Relations are warm and cooperative. Both countries share values on rule of law, climate action, and multilateralism. Svezia's NATO accession strengthened the bilateral security relationship. Some divergence on EU fiscal policy (Svezia leans more fiscally conservative).

military.summary: Svezia joined NATO in March 2024, bringing it into formal alliance with Francia. Swedish Gripen fighters and French Rafale are European-built alternatives. Cooperation in Baltic security, NATO's eastern flank, and joint exercises increasing post-2024. SAAB and French defence firms have some collaborative relationships.

financial.summary: Significant bilateral investment flows. Major Swedish companies (IKEA, H&M, Volvo, Electrolux) have large French operations. French companies invest in Svezia's industrial and energy sectors. Both countries have open, transparent investment environments.

energy.summary: Both countries have low-carbon electricity systems (Francia: nuclear; Svezia: hydropower and nuclear). Cooperation in offshore wind (Baltic Sea) and hydrogen. Svezia exports clean electricity to continental Europe via Denmark-Germania links that benefit Francia indirectly.

scientific.summary: Strong research collaboration through EU frameworks. Cooperation in life sciences (AstraZeneca, Karolinska links to French pharma research), materials science, and climate. Swedish and French universities have extensive exchange programmes. CERN and ESA cooperation robust.
